----------- Embedding ----------
An embedding is a numerical vector representation of data such as:

--> Text
--> Images
--> Audio
--> PDFs
--> Code
The embedding captures the semantic meaning of the content.

# Example:
"I love dogs"

# might become:

[0.12, -0.45, 0.91, 0.33, ...]

# These numbers are generated by embedding models such as:
--> OpenAI embeddings
--> Google Gemini embeddings
--> Meta embeddings
--> Ollama local models

# Do We Store Embeddings
Embeddings are stored so applications can:

--> Search documents semantically
--> Retrieve similar content
--> Build RAG systems
--> Power chatbots
--> Recommend products
--> Detect duplicates
--> Cluster similar data
Without storing embeddings, you would need to regenerate them every time, which is expensive and 
slow.

# Traditional Search vs Embedding Search
--> Traditional Keyword Search
    Searches exact words.

    Example:
    Query: "car"

    Finds documents containing:
    car

    But misses:
    automobile
    vehicle

# Embedding-Based Semantic Search
Embeddings understand meaning.

Query:
"car"

Can retrieve:
automobile
vehicle
SUV
transportation
because their embeddings are mathematically close.

# Embeddings Are Stored
Embeddings are stored as vectors inside:

--> Vector databases
--> In-memory indexes
--> SQL databases
--> NoSQL databases
--> Flat files

# Structure of Stored Embeddings
Usually each stored item contains:

{
    "id": "doc_1",
    "text": "Artificial Intelligence is transforming industries",
    "embedding": [0.12, 0.44, -0.91, ...],
    "metadata": {
        "source": "AI_Book.pdf",
        "page": 12
    }
}


Components of Embedding Storage

1. Original Content
The actual text/document chunk.

Example:
"Machine learning is a subset of AI"


2. Embedding Vector
High-dimensional numeric representation.

Example:
[0.21, -0.11, 0.88, ...]

Dimensions may be:

384
768
1024
1536
3072
depending on model.

3. Metadata
Extra information about the chunk.

Example:

{
    "pdf": "ml_book.pdf",
    "chapter": 2,
    "page": 15
}

Metadata helps filtering during search.


Special databases optimized for vector similarity search.

Popular vector databases:

Database	                Description
ChromaDB	                Beginner friendly
FAISS	                    Fast local similarity search
Pinecone	                Cloud vector DB
Weaviate	                Open-source vector DB
Milvus	                    Scalable vector search
Qdrant	                    High-performance vector DB
